rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or properly declared as helpers.
Resolve type mismatches by aligning expression types with declared helper types (int, real, bool).
Avoid placing invalid guarantees or assignments in implementation scopes; use only valid output features or declared variables.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced identifiers are resolvable within the current scope.
Use only declared constants, variables, or visible features in AGREE expressions to prevent '<error>' type issues.
Validate that scalar type names in AGREE match supported types: int, real, bool.
Prefer deterministic input-output relationships in component type contracts to avoid implementation-specific dependencies.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assignments in implementation scopes must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Guarantee statements should not be placed in implementation-level annexes unless they refer to visible outputs.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers that are not present in the model's configured dependencies.
Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type annexes unless represented through valid variables or outputs.
Do not place invalid or untyped expressions in AGREE contracts without resolving root causes.
